Description: Supplies leading identifiers up to the hierarchy level specified by the index, for example, "A.UA.UA2" for index 3 for a function designation "A.UA.UA2.UA3.UA4".
Supplies leading identifiers up to the hierarchy level specified by the index, for example, "O.UO.UO2" for index 3 for a location designation "O.UO.UO2.UO3.UO4".
This property can be used in forms (for example for the Table of contents), but is not designed for use in filters.
This property can be used in forms (for example for the Table of contents), but is not designed for use in filters.
Defines the page type, i.e. whether it is a page for schematics, mounting panels, terminal diagrams, etc.

Designation: Structure identifier with separators stored in the object identifier
Description: The unique structure identifier without leading separator stored in the object identifier. An empty string is output either if no valid structure identifier is stored in the object identifier or if several structure identifiers are stored. This property serves for display purposes in the page navigator.

Designation: Part of the structure identifier without separators stored in the object identifier
Description: Supplies a part of the unique structure identifier without leading separator stored in the object identifier. An empty string is output either if no valid structure identifier is stored in the object identifier or if several structure identifiers are stored. This property serves to display in the page navigator.

Designation: Contains results in the search results list
Description: With the help of this property the pages contained in the search results list can be taken into consideration when pages are filtered.
Outputs the internal index value of the associated project revision (from change tracking) on a page; max. 1,000 entries are allowed.
